mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in monthly retail and food service sales indicates robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC <= 0 over any 2-year window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS is at an all-time high.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, causing negative equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: If triggered instances show >50% positive 5-day returns, or if UNRATE >5.0 leads to positive SPX in more than half of cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus year-over-year CPI inflation) is negative, accommodative monetary policy compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If rolling 20-day returns are not significantly positive when real rate is negative (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in manufacturing output, which precedes earnings revisions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If triggered periods show median 60-day return > 0, or if the signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.3 million annualized units signals a contraction in residential investment, dragging down GDP and equity markets over the medium term.
failure_condition: If median 60-day return after trigger is positive, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Continued growth in nonfarm payroll employment indicates a strong labor market supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS is in the top decile of monthly changes but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP and industrial production are above their respective 12-month moving averages, the economy is expanding robustly, boosting corporate earnings and stock prices over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both above 12-month MA
failure_condition: If the joint signal yields a negative average forward return, or if the Sharpe ratio of the signal-based strategy is below 0 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 4% and payroll employment exceeds 147 million, the labor market is extremely tight, creating wage inflation pressure that leads the Fed to tighten, causing negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If during such tight labor market regimes, the S&P 500 produces positive median 60-day returns, or if the negative signal fails to beat a random classifier over any 5-year window.
